title: Selliguea polysora
taxonomy: Selliguea polysora is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Selliguea, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical regions. The genus Selliguea is further grouped into the order Polypodiales, which includes various species of ferns with multiple leaflets. Selliguea polysora is native to Southeast Asia and is often found growing in shaded areas. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics, including its frond and leaflet arrangement.
uses: Selliguea polysora, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, has been reported to have various medicinal uses. Its leaves have been traditionally used to treat fever and inflammation. intro: Selliguea polysora, also known as Selaginella polyspora or Spikemoss, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Selaginellaceae, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The health benefits of Selliguea polysora include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include saponins, flavonoids, and other glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
